ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUN 22/23, 2026 · BUILT MON JUN 22 ~17:55 ET

ES Trading Plan — Mon ETH & Tue RTH

Charts: TPO profile (Jun 22 last RTH) + 30-min RTH/ETH + Daily + VX1! (17:49–17:55 ET) · Catalyst: equities dragged off record highs as megacaps (Alphabet -10%, hyperscalers Amazon/Oracle/Meta -2%+) sold on AI-capex worry; chips (Micron/Sandisk +5%) still bid — a rotation, not a flush · Rising Treasury yields a headwind · Brent settled < $78 on US–Iran peace progress (two-month deal framework) · MOC net BUY +$1,122M S&P / +$440M NDX / −$328M Dow
Last 7,531.50 (ETH 14:05; daily C 7,533.00)
Jun 22 RTH: O 7,573.25 · H 7,599.25 · L 7,527.25 · C 7,540.25
Jun 22 POC 7,538.50 · VAH 7,554.25 · VAL 7,527.25
Poor low 7,527.25 · single print 7,570.75 · weak high 7,599.25
vs 20-EMA 7,525.00 +6.50 (sitting on it)
vs 50-EMA 7,403.25 +128.25 · ATR 102.50
VIX 18.50 ▲0.05 (flat-low; orderly)
▼ What changed since the Jun 22 (for Jun 22/23) plan — record-high rejection, value rolled LOWER, regime is megacap-rotation not broad risk-off The prior plan looked for a repair UP to the Jun 18 poor high 7,581.50. Instead Monday Jun 22 opened 7,573.25, tagged 7,599.25 (a weak/poor high, just under the 2D selling tail 7,605.75), and was sold all day, closing 7,540.25 — inside value but at the lower end, right on the POC 7,538.50. Value migrated LOWER (VAH 7,554.25 / POC 7,538.50 / VAL 7,527.25 vs the prior 7,571.50/7,561.75/7,549.00). The session left a poor low at 7,527.25 (unfinished, flat base — repair-down magnet), a single print 7,570.75 (speed zone overhead), and rejected the 7,599 weak high. Price now sits at 7,531.50 in early ETH, parked on the daily 20-EMA (7,525.00) — the first real test of trend support since the FOMC flush was repaired. Catalyst read: this was a megacap/AI-hyperscaler rotation, not a flush — Alphabet −10% and Amazon/Oracle/Meta −2%+ on AI-capex worry dragged the cap-weighted tape, while chips (Micron/Sandisk +5%) and value (BofA/JPM +2%) held bid; the Dow was nearly green. Rising Treasury yields are the persistent overhead. Iran de-escalation continues (two-month peace framework; Brent < $78), so the geopolitical tail has cooled — the swing factor is now AI-capex sentiment + yields. VIX 18.50 barely moved on a down day = orderly repricing, not fear → lean less bearish on the structure. MOC was a net BUY (+$1.12B S&P) — dip demand into the close despite the red tape. Drawn labels read directly: Today RTH Weak High 7,599.25, Single Print 7,570.75, VAH 7,554.25, POC 7,538.50, Poor Low 7,527.25; Prev RTH Single Print 7,514.25, Spike Base 7,496.25, VAL 7,463.50; fib extension 7,621.75 (up) / 7,396.50 (down).

Shape: a P-fade / b-shaped distribution — built up early, sold the 7,599 weak high, then accepted value lower with the POC migrating down to 7,538.50. Features: poor low 7,527.25 (flat, unfinished — high probability of a repair-down test), single print 7,570.75 (overhead speed zone), weak/poor high 7,599.25 (rejected, repair magnet). Value overlapped LOWER two sessions running — sellers have the short-term initiative, but price still closed inside value above POC and on a net-buy MOC, so it's distribution-within-balance, not breakdown.

STRUCTURE MAP (ETH ref 7,531.50) 7,621.75 ── fib ext up 7,599.25 ── weak high (rejected) ▲ overhead supply 7,570.75 ── single print (speed zone) 7,554.25 ── VAH 7,538.50 ── POC ◄ fair value / close 7,531.50 ●● SPOT 7,527.25 ── poor low / VAL ◄ first test 7,525.00 ── 20-EMA (daily) 7,514.25 ── prev single print (shelf) ▼ responsive-buy shelves 7,496.25 ── spike base 7,463.50 ── prev VAL 7,396.50 ── fib ext down

d) VIX & macro

VX1! — 18.50, flat on a down day (orderly repricing, melt regime intact); range low ~17.85

VIX 18.50, essentially flat (+0.05) on a down equity day with the cap-weighted index dragged off records — the textbook signature of orderly rotation, not fear. Range low ~17.85. As long as VIX holds < 19–20, the melt/buy-dips template stays intact and structure leans less bearish than the red tape suggests. Macro: AI-capex sentiment (Alphabet −10%, hyperscalers soft) + rising Treasury yields are the active headwinds; chips and value rotated UP; Iran de-escalation (two-month framework, Brent < $78) has removed the geopolitical tail. MOC net BUY +$1.12B S&P = institutional dip demand into the close.

09Structural Warnings — ETH

⚠ Poor low 7,527.25 — repair-down magnetA flat, unfinished base. ETH frequently repairs poor lows: expect a probe through 7,527 toward 7,514 even if price ultimately holds. Structure stops below 7,520, not at 7,527.
⚠ Single print 7,570.75 — overhead speed zoneThin auction: price travels through it fast both ways. Don't fade inside it; trade the edges (enter on the reclaim above or the rejection below).
⚠ Weak high 7,599.25 + selling tail 7,605.75 — first-tag rejection bandRepair magnet that rejects the first tag. Only 2× 30-min closes > 7,605.75 open the 7,621 fib / ATH path. Don't chase a first push into it.
ℹ 7,514 / 7,496 — back-filled support, not airThe FOMC-flush structure below is fully repaired and reads as responsive-buy shelves, not speed zones. These are entries on a deeper retrace, not danger.
⚠ Yields + AI-capex are the live tailThis pullback was megacap/AI-capex + rising-yield driven, not geopolitical. A fresh yield spike or capex scare (vs Wed/Thu earnings, auctions) is the gap risk — watch the 10-yr and the VIX gate.
ℹ VIX gate< 19 = orderly drift/buy-dips (favor longs at shelves) · 19–21 = two-sided, respect both edges · > 21 = template suspended, responsive longs only at 7,496/7,463.

🔗 Controlling clusters7,527.25 poor low + 7,525.00 20-EMA (4-pt band) = the make-or-break shelf for Tuesday. Overhead, a dense supply stack 7,565 / 7,570.75 / 7,599.25 / 7,605.75 caps rallies — the 7,565–7,571 band is the first ceiling, 7,599–7,606 the second. R1 POC 7,538.50 is the immediate pivot the open will fight over.

05Probability Matrix — RTH

CombinedBull 73% · Bear 27%100%
Base case & reasoningR1 + R2 (dip-buy the shelf, 54% combined). In a rising-MA-stack bull trend, even Monday's "bear" distribution resolves as a dip-buy locator unless a macro regime shift confirms — and the tells lean constructive: net-buy MOC, flat VIX (orderly rotation, not fear), chips/value rotating up, and the Iran tail removed. Genuine directional downside (R5) requires a fresh yields/AI-capex catalyst to close price below the 7,514 shelf; absent that, the 7,527/7,525/7,514 shelves are buy locations, not breakdown levels. Shorts (R3) are tactical fades at the overhead supply stack, not trend trades.

Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 102.50 but Monday's range was compressed (72 pts): start 55–65% size; the longs (R1/R2) are the higher-conviction side in a rising-MA / dip-buy regime, the supply fade (R3) is a ¾-size tactical scalp. Hard rule: any rising-yields or AI-capex headline = flat longs first, reassess — a 30-min close < 7,514 is the line that turns dips from buys into breakdowns.

09Structural Warnings — RTH

⚠ Poor low 7,527.25 — repair-down magnetFlat, unfinished base = high probability of a probe through it toward 7,514 even on a constructive day. Structure stops below 7,520, not at 7,527; treat the first break as a look-below-and-fail until 7,514 closes below.
⚠ Single print 7,570.75 — overhead speed zoneThin auction; price fills it fast. Don't fade inside it — trade the reclaim above (long) or the rejection below (short). It's the gate to the 7,591/7,599 supply.
⚠ Weak high 7,599.25 + selling tail 7,605.75 — first-tag rejectionRepair magnet that fades the first tag. Only 2× 30-min closes > 7,605.75 open the 7,621 fib / ATH. Don't chase the first push.
⚠ Structural void below 7,514If 7,514 closes below, there's thinner structure into 7,508 → 7,496; a yields/AI headline can travel this quickly. No-chase zone — wait for the 7,496 spike base for a responsive long.
ℹ Extension from POC — none yetPrice is AT fair value (−7 from POC), so there's no mean-reversion stretch in either direction at the open; the edge is at the shelf (buy) and the supply stack (fade), not at the POC itself.
ℹ VIX / yields gateVIX < 19 + 10-yr stable = dip-buy (favor R1/R2) · VIX 19–21 or yields rising = two-sided, tighten · VIX > 21 = R5 in play, responsive longs only at 7,496/7,463.
